events.name: bash

input.description: Fetch PR review comments with IDs

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: replace `formatOutput(data: any)` with a narrowed union like `formatOutput(data: Record<string, unknown> | unknown[])` (or a generic `formatOutput<T>(data: T)`). That removes `any` while still accepting JSON-serializable inputs.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: wrap `JSON.parse` in a `try/catch` and return a safe result envelope, e.g. `{ ok: true, data } | { ok: false, error }`, so callers can handle invalid JSON without throwing at runtime.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: implement schema validation instead of returning `true` (e.g. Ajv `validate(schema, data)` and return `validate.errors` on failure), or remove this helper and validate at the call site if schema checks are out of scope.

events.content:
Done — I went through PR `#429` and replied to all unresolved review threads only (3 total), each with a concrete fix suggestion.

I did not push any code changes.
